test.name: Generate Ancestry search URL for the 1870 census
test.description: Single-turn decomposition (per unit-test-spec.md §1): test the URL-generation phase only. The skill should produce a pre-filled Ancestry search URL with the right parameters and capture-workflow instructions. The capture-analysis phase (multi-turn) is tested via a separate record-extraction test. The 1850 Ancestry search is already completed in mid-research-flynn (pli_002/log_002); the 1870 census is a genuine gap with no existing plan item, so the skill should proceed with URL generation rather than asking permission to re-run.

input.user_message: Search for Patrick Flynn on Ancestry's 1870 census.

judge_context: Generated URL should target Ancestry.com (or the appropriate ancestry.{tld}) and include search parameters derived from the subject: name 'Patrick Flynn', birth year ~1845, and birthplace Ireland. NOTE: the scenario's birthplace conflict is RESOLVED to Ireland (conflicts[0]: the 1850/1860 censuses are accepted; the 1908 death certificate's 'Pennsylvania' — which the project objective still echoes — was rejected as son-in-law informant error). Using 'Ireland' is therefore correct and matches the resolved state; using 'Pennsylvania' would contradict it. Do not penalize 'Ireland' as an unsupported claim

Should provide capture-workflow instructions: tell the user to save matching results as a PDF and paste back the file path or content, with named record-type expectations (1870 census household entry — Patrick would be ~25 by then, possibly head of household if married)
Should not invent record content or pre-fill captured results — the analyze phase happens only after the user returns the capture
